text.title: Turborepo Setup — Directory Layout and Configuration
text.summary: Canonical monorepo structure with apps/, packages/, pnpm workspaces, and turbo.json v2 task graph.

rule.statement: Place applications under apps/ and shared packages under packages/; never mix app and library code in the same directory.
rule.rationale: Turborepo's filter syntax and task graph require a predictable location to distinguish deployable apps from reusable packages.

example.code: monorepo/ ├── turbo.json ├── package.json ├── pnpm-workspace.yaml ├── apps/ │ ├── web/ (Next.js frontend) │ └── api/ (backend service) └── packages/ ├── ui/ (shared React components) ├── config/ (eslint-preset, tsconfig/) ├── types/ (shared TypeScript types) └── utils/ (shared utilities)

rule.statement: Declare workspace globs in pnpm-workspace.yaml and use workspace:* for all internal dependencies — never floating semver ranges.
rule.rationale: workspace:* resolves to local symlinks; floating ranges can accidentally resolve to a published npm version.

section.title: turbo.json v2 Task Graph

rule.statement: Use the tasks field (not pipeline) in turbo.json v2; always declare outputs for every task — even [] for side-effect-only tasks.
rule.rationale: Turborepo 2.x renamed pipeline to tasks. Missing outputs means the task is never cached; Turborepo runs it on every invocation.

section.reference: Minimal turbo.json using tasks field with build/lint/typecheck/test/dev.

example.note: v1 pipeline field and missing outputs; both are silent failures in v2.

rule.statement: Pin packageManager to a specific pnpm version in root package.json and set engines.node to enforce the runtime version.
rule.rationale: Mismatched pnpm versions produce lockfile churn; mismatched Node versions cause silent behavior differences.
